stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM from Meta.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its current popularity and performance tracking.
The context involves standard NLP pipelines like text-generation, relevant to the broader AI community.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/AI ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
The model utilizes transformers and safetensors, indicating its technical foundation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, promising faster local AI inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and control.
The tool leverages local AI infrastructure (implied by Ollama context) to run AI agents privately.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
The partnership involves Ollama, OpenAI, and ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools).

stories.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
stories.author: Mia Sato
bullet_points: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection tool to all adult users (18+).
The feature utilizes a selfie-style scan to monitor the platform for potential deepfakes of the user.
If a likeness match is found, YouTube will issue an alert to the user.

stories.title: The US is betting on AI to catch insider trading in prediction markets
stories.author: Kate Knibbs, wired.com
bullet_points: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is actively using AI to combat insider trading.
The focus of this AI application is monitoring prediction markets for illicit trading activities.
This signals a significant regulatory shift towards leveraging advanced AI for financial market integrity.

stories.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement is getting messy as judge delays approval
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic is involved in a significant copyright settlement valued at $1.5 billion.
The approval of this historic settlement is facing delays from the judge.
Legal disputes have arisen, with accusations that lawyers were rushing the settlement to secure substantial fees.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework designed to update Large Language Models (LLMs) with new, domain-specific knowledge.
The core innovation is encoding new knowledge into a dedicated memory model without altering the original LLM parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being 'frozen' after pretraining, making them more suitable for real-world, timely applications.

stories.title: SANA-WM, a 2.6B open-source world model for 1-minute 720p video
stories.author: mjgil
bullet_points: Introduction of SANA-WM, a new open-source world model.
The model is capable of generating 1-minute, 720p video content.
It represents a significant step in open-source generative AI/ML research.

stories.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
stories.author: gemini-enterprise
bullet_points: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite has been released and is now generally available.
The model is positioned as the fastest and most cost-efficient option.
Availability is specifically on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of 'subagents' for more complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added to Claude Code via Ollama integration.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-weight models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok settle suit over harm to students
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok have settled a lawsuit regarding the impact of social media on students.
The suit alleged that social media addiction has caused financial strain and disrupted learning in public schools.
The case, initiated by the Breathitt County School District, centers on mental health crises linked to social media use.

stories.title: Three's a party: US, China, and now Russia are on the prowl in GEO
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The article discusses the increasing surveillance activity by the US, China, and Russia in Geosynchronous Orbit (GEO).
It highlights a technical vulnerability: many satellites are not as stealthy as assumed, being visible against the blackness of space.
The focus is on the growing geopolitical competition and monitoring capabilities in space.

stories.title: EntityBench: Towards Entity-Consistent Long-Range Multi-Shot Video Generation
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Introduces EntityBench, a new benchmark for multi-shot video generation.
Addresses the key challenge of maintaining entity consistency (characters, objects, locations) over long video sequences.
The benchmark is derived from real narrative media, offering a more robust evaluation standard than previous methods.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, open-ended environments.
The core innovation is replaying real-world events chronologically to provide realistic, grounded testing scenarios.
Agents use FutureSim to forecast future world events beyond their initial knowledge cutoff while interacting with the historical timeline.

stories.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
stories.author: Wentao Zhang
bullet_points: VGGT-Edit introduces a new method for 3D scene editing using a feed-forward native approach.
It addresses the limitation of current high-quality 3D reconstruction models, which struggle with dynamic human instructions.
The research moves beyond traditional 2D-lifting strategies for scene modification.

stories.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
stories.author: Xueyan Zou
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of quantitatively evaluating the 3D geometric plausibility of videos generated by AI world models.
Introduces PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a new quantitative framework for auditing geometric coherence.
Moves beyond subjective human judgment or learned graders to provide a more diagnostic evaluation method for generative video models.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
Identifies gradient conflict and optimization instability when co-training disparate PDE regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media flows).
Proposes a solution using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible spectral and geometric demands.

stories.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces OpenDeepThink, a new framework for improving LLM reasoning by scaling breadth rather than just depth.
Addresses the 'selection bottleneck' in parallel reasoning by avoiding noisy, pointwise LLM judging.
Uses pairwise Bradley-Terry aggregation to select the best reasoning candidate from a population of parallel traces.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening.
Addresses limitations in current medical image models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical case referencing.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to improve screening accuracy and transparency.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between narrative and structured data for better patient trajectory modeling.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: The paper argues that current behavioral assurance methods are insufficient to verify complex AI safety claims.
AI governance frameworks demand verifiable evidence for properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability.
Current assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are limited by what they can observe in model output.

stories.title: RefDecoder: Enhancing Visual Generation with Conditional Video Decoding
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in latent diffusion models where decoders are often unconditional despite heavily conditioned denoising networks.
Proposes RefDecoder, an architecture designed to provide equal conditioning to the decoder to maintain structural integrity in video generation.
Focuses on improving the detail and consistency of generated video content for various downstream applications.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agent adaptability in dynamic environments.
The core innovation is replaying real-world events chronologically, allowing agents to interact with historical data streams.
This enables agents to forecast future events beyond their initial knowledge cutoff while maintaining chronological context.

stories.title: When Are Two Networks the Same? Tensor Similarity for Mechanistic Interpretability
stories.author: Thomas Dooms
bullet_points: Introduces 'tensor similarity,' a new weight-based metric for comparing neural network components.
Addresses limitations of existing similarity measures by being invariant to weight-space symmetries and out-of-distribution behavior.
Supports the field of mechanistic interpretability, which seeks to understand the internal computations of AI models.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
Identifies gradient conflict and optimization instability when co-training disparate PDE regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media flows).
Proposes a solution, 'Shod' (Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing), to manage incompatible spectral and geometric demands in dense neural operators.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening.
Addresses limitations in current medical image models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical case referencing.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to improve screening accuracy and transparency.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It proposes a multimodal alignment approach (Retrieval-Augmented) to combine rich narrative text with precise structured EHR data.
The application is focused on complex conditions like sepsis, aiming to improve patient trajectory modeling and risk forecasting.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: The paper argues that current behavioral assurance methods are insufficient to verify complex AI safety claims.
AI governance frameworks demand evidence for properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability.
Current assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are epistemically limited to observable model outputs.

stories.title: Hand-in-the-Loop: Improving Dexterous VLA via Seamless Interventional Correction
stories.author: Ruoshi Wen
bullet_points: The research addresses compounding errors in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models during complex, dexterous robotic manipulation.
A key challenge identified is the command mismatch when using Interactive Imitation Learning (IIL) for high-DoF robotic hands during human takeover.
The proposed 'Hand-in-the-Loop' method aims to improve VLA performance by enabling seamless interventional correction.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yongliang Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of coarse, trajectory-level reward signals in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM agents.
It introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) to provide dense, token-level guidance using a teacher branch with privileged context.
The core challenge identified is the instability of transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents due to compounding supervision issues.

stories.title: I Added Three Rules to Gemma 4. The MoE Searched. The Dense Model Refused.
stories.author: alimafana
bullet_points: The article compares the performance of Gemma 4 (MoE vs. Dense) against GPT-4o/GPT-4o mini in a real-world Arabic e-commerce chatbot scenario.
A key finding suggests architectural differences matter, as the MoE variant successfully navigated a catalog while the dense variant failed with the same prompt.
The experiment highlights the impact of specific, model-specific prompt rules on the behavior and capability of different LLM architectures.

stories.title: I Built an AI Agent Risk Auditor for Agentic Repositories
stories.author: james_bridges_5511048e2cf
bullet_points: Introduction of 'Hermes Guard,' a new AI Agent Risk Auditor.
The tool is designed as a local-first solution to audit repositories modified by AI agents.
It features evidence-backed reporting and a safe-by-design scanning mechanism.
